description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a minimal deployment bundle for a fleet of IoT devices.

    1. A single-byte “firmware image” already exists at
       /home/user/sample_firmware_v1.2.bin.  
       (It is exactly one byte long and contains the lowercase letter “a”.)

    2. Create the directory hierarchy
       /home/user/iot_device/builds  
       (create all missing parent directories as needed).

    3. Move the file /home/user/sample_firmware_v1.2.bin into the newly-created
       /home/user/iot_device/builds directory and rename it to firmware.bin.

    4. Inside /home/user/iot_device/builds generate a file named SHA256SUMS
       that contains ONE line only, produced with the standard sha256sum
       utility, in the *precise* format

       <64-hex-digit-checksum><two spaces>firmware.bin\n

       Example of correct layout (DON’T copy this hash, it is only a sample):
       d2d2…d2  firmware.bin

       Important formatting rules the test will check:
       • Exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.  
       • Exactly TWO spaces between the checksum and the filename.  
       • The filename must be firmware.bin.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline.

    5. Create a deployment log at
       /home/user/iot_device/deploy.log.  
       It must contain exactly two lines, **no extra blank lines**, with the
       following literal text:

       FIRMWARE_MOVED:OK  
       CHECKSUM_CREATED:OK

    Verification notes for yourself:
       • Paths must be absolute as specified.  
       • The original file must no longer exist at its old location after the
         move.  
       • The checksum recorded in SHA256SUMS must match the actual SHA-256
         digest of the final firmware.bin.

    You are free to use any standard shell utilities, but keep the solution
    to a simple set of 3–4 commands.
truth:
Initial state:
      • File /home/user/sample_firmware_v1.2.bin exists.
        Content (hex): 0x61   (ASCII “a”, no newline)
      • No /home/user/iot_device directory is present.

    Expected final state:

      1. Directory /home/user/iot_device/builds exists.

      2. /home/user/iot_device/builds/firmware.bin  
         • Size: 1 byte  
         • Content (hex): 0x61

      3. /home/user/iot_device/builds/SHA256SUMS  
         • Content (exact, incl. trailing newline):  
           ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb␠␠firmware.bin\n

      4. /home/user/iot_device/deploy.log  
         • Content (two lines, Unix LF endings):  
           FIRMWARE_MOVED:OK\n  
           CHECKSUM_CREATED:OK\n

      5. File /home/user/sample_firmware_v1.2.bin no longer exists.

    Notes for test harness:
      • Verify SHA-256 of firmware.bin equals
        ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb.
      • Verify SHA256SUMS line matches the pattern
        “^ca978112…48bb␠␠firmware\.bin\\n$”.
      • Confirm deploy.log has exactly two lines as specified.
name: task_000000_62c7991d
